recipeBook.note: What I actually cook. Most of it goes on the grill out back. Chloe gets a say on her weekends.

recipe.name: Coffee Rubbed Ribeye

steps.step: Mix the rub, press it on both sides, let the steaks sit out 40 minutes.
Grill hot, about 4 minutes a side for medium, do not poke at them.
Rest 5 minutes before cutting or the juice runs out.

recipe.notes: Chloe likes hers cut into strips. Cold beer, not for her.

recipe.name: Grilled Chicken Thighs

steps.step: Marinade in a bag at least two hours, all day is better.
Medium grill, skin side down first, turn once, pull at 175 in the thigh.
Leftovers go in burritos through the week.

recipe.notes: Cheap, feeds the week, hard to mess up.

recipe.name: Weeknight Chili

steps.step: Brown the beef and onion, drain.
Everything in the pot, simmer low an hour and a half.
Cheddar on top. Better the second day.

recipe.notes: Makes enough for three dinners. Freezes fine.

recipe.name: Breakfast Burritos

steps.step: Scramble soft, fold in the meat and cheese.
Roll tight, wrap in foil, they hold heat to the lake.
Ray brings these half the time anyway.

recipe.notes: Make extra and freeze. Microwave 90 seconds on a workday.

recipe.name: Saturday Spaghetti

steps.step: Brown the beef, stir in the sauce and garlic, simmer.
Boil the pasta, save a splash of the water for the sauce.

recipe.notes: The backup when nobody wants to wait on the grill.
